seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Clifton Springs and Clonbinane. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Clifton Springs vs Clonbinane: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Clifton Springs and Clonbinane.
heading: Clifton Springs vs Clonbinane — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Clifton Springs, Victoria and Clonbinane, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Clifton Springs has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Clonbinane (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Clifton Springs or Clonbinane safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Clifton Springs has a flood risk score of 27/100, while Clonbinane scores 29/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Clifton Springs scores 54/100 for bushfire risk. Clonbinane scores 53/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Clifton Springs or Clonbinane?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Clifton Springs has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.2% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
